seoTitle: Logie Brae vs Long Beach — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Logie Brae and Long Beach. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Logie Brae vs Long Beach: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Logie Brae and Long Beach.
heading: Logie Brae vs Long Beach — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Logie Brae, New South Wales and Long Beach, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Long Beach has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to Logie Brae (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Logie Brae or Long Beach safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Logie Brae has a flood risk score of 47/100, while Long Beach scores 32/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Logie Brae scores 34/100 for bushfire risk. Long Beach scores 51/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Logie Brae or Long Beach?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Long Beach has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.2% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
